name: DROID X7
description: DROID expander. USB-C + TRS MIDI in/out with polyphonic mapping, four extra gate outs at modular levels, USB access to the master's SD card, sub-second sysex patch upload from the Forge.

controls.name: Mode switch
controls.description: Three-position slide switch selecting the function of the USB-C port.
controls.detail: left: SD card USB-stick mode (patch stops) · middle: off · right: USB-MIDI active

controls.name: USB-C port
controls.description: Class-compliant USB. Appears as mass-storage or MIDI device depending on switch.
controls.detail: requires Rev 1.5.1 for USB-C↔USB-C · older revs need USB-A cable

controls.name: LED indicators
controls.description: Multicolor LEDs show SD card activity, USB-MIDI state, TRS MIDI in/out, and the four gate outputs.
controls.detail: top-left: SD · top-right: USB-MIDI · middle: TRS in/out · bottom: gate 9–12

inputs.name: MIDI IN (TRS)
inputs.description: 3.5mm TRS MIDI input with autosensing for MIDI TRS type A and B. Accessed in patch via midiin circuit.

outputs.name: MIDI OUT (TRS)
outputs.description: 3.5mm TRS MIDI output. Switch must be middle or right for DIN adapter use. Accessed via midiout circuit.

outputs.name: G9 / G10 / G11 / G12
outputs.description: Four dedicated gate/trigger outputs at modular level. Addressed in patch as G9..G12 on a MASTER, or the next available G index on MASTER18.

behaviors.name: Always first in the chain
behaviors.description: The X7 connects to the MASTER's 6-pin controller header and MUST be the first module in the controller chain. Any P2B8, P10, B32, M4 etc. chain off the X7's OUT connector. Only one X7 per master. The X7 is not declared in the patch — it's auto-detected — and does not consume a controller number, so P1.1 still refers to the first non-X7 controller.

behaviors.name: USB-stick mode stops the patch
behaviors.description: In the left position the master exposes its SD card as a mass-storage device to the host PC. While in this mode the patch is NOT running — outputs freeze. Return switch to middle to re-mount the patch and resume. Useful for grabbing ERRORS.TXT, STATES1.TXT or writing droid.ini directly.

behaviors.name: Forge sysex activate
behaviors.description: With the switch right the X7 is a class-compliant USB MIDI device. Forge's Activate! button uploads the patch via sysex in under a second, with no SD card eject. The only interactive DROID development workflow; without an X7 every iteration requires SD card swapping.

behaviors.name: Polyphonic MIDI in
behaviors.description: midiin assigns incoming note events across as many voice slots as outputs declared. Supports note on/off, CC, pitch bend, aftertouch, clock, running state, pedals. USB and TRS streams are independent — two simultaneous input streams.

behaviors.name: CV-to-MIDI with stabilization
behaviors.description: midiout converts CV pitches and gates to MIDI. Built-in pitch-stabilization detection eliminates micro-variations from Eurorack sequencers/quantizers, so sequenced melodies translate cleanly to MIDI notes without jitter retriggers.

behaviors.name: Four extra gate outputs
behaviors.description: Frees master O1–O8 from gate-duty. Outputs 0V/5V. Use for drum triggers, clock distribution, envelope gates. Addressed just like G8 expander jacks but always available as long as the X7 is connected, regardless of G8 presence.

behaviors.name: MIDI through routing
behaviors.description: midithrough forwards events from one MIDI stream to another with selectable filters — let USB notes pass to TRS out, split CCs, merge USB+TRS into one bus, etc. Events can be spliced in and out without stopping the native midiin/midiout behavior.

behaviors.name: Not a USB host
behaviors.description: The X7 is a USB device, not a host. USB-only MIDI keyboards (no DIN, no TRS) cannot be connected to the X7 via USB. Use the TRS MIDI IN with the supplied DIN adapter instead. The USB port is for connection to PC/Mac/tablet/phone only.

patchIdeas.name: MIDI keyboard to 4-voice CV
patchIdeas.patch: midiin circuit declares 4 voices · pitch outputs routed to O1–O4, gates to G9–G12 · last-note priority or round-robin voice assignment via midiin parameters · plug MIDI keyboard to X7 TRS IN via supplied DIN adapter · play four-note chords driving an external 4-voice polysynth from the master.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DROID:
* midiin: channel=1, voices=4, pitch1=O1, pitch2=O2, pitch3=O3, pitch4=O4, gate1=G9, gate2=G10, gate3=G11, gate4=G12

- MIDI Keyboard (MIDI Out) -> DROID X7 (MIDI IN)
- DROID (O1) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- DROID (O2) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- DROID (O3) p> VCO 3 (V/Oct)
- DROID (O4) p> VCO 4 (V/Oct)
- DROID X7 (G9) g> VCA 1 Env (Gate)
- DROID X7 (G10) g> VCA 2 Env (Gate)
- DROID X7 (G11) g> VCA 3 Env (Gate)
- DROID X7 (G12) g> VCA 4 Env (Gate)

patchIdeas.name: CV to DAW (MIDI out)
patchIdeas.patch: Eurorack sequencer V/oct → I1 · gate → I2 · midiout converts to note-on/off on USB channel 1 · X7 switch RIGHT · DAW records a clean MIDI performance with stabilized pitch (no jitter retriggers) · modular plays, DAW captures.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DROID:
* midiout: channel=1, pitch1=I1, gate1=I2

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> DROID (I1)
- Sequencer (Gate) g> DROID (I2)
- DROID X7 (USB-C) -> DAW (USB In)

patchIdeas.name: Four-gate clock distribution
patchIdeas.patch: lfo → _CLK at 120 BPM · clocktool divides _CLK by 1, 2, 4, 8 into G9–G12 · modular gets four synchronized clock lanes without consuming any of the master's O outputs · master's CV outs remain free for pitch/modulation.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DROID:
* lfo: hz=2, square=_CLK
* clocktool: input=_CLK, output=G9
* clocktool: input=_CLK, divide=2, output=G10
* clocktool: input=_CLK, divide=4, output=G11
* clocktool: input=_CLK, divide=8, output=G12

- DROID X7 (G9) c> Drum 1 (Trig)
- DROID X7 (G10) c> Drum 2 (Trig)
- DROID X7 (G11) c> Drum 3 (Trig)
- DROID X7 (G12) c> Drum 4 (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Forge-driven live coding
patchIdeas.patch: Switch set RIGHT · Forge open on laptop · edit circuits live and hit Activate! · patch reloads in <1s · sequencers restart with saved state preserved in DROIDSTA.BIN · iterative DROID development with no SD card fiddling · workflow the X7 was designed for.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DROID X7:
* switch: RIGHT

- Laptop (Forge) -> DROID X7 (USB-C) // sysex Activate!

patchIdeas.name: MIDI clock slave
patchIdeas.patch: midiin reads MIDI clock from external hardware · clock output → G9 at 24 PPQ · running-state flag → G10 (high while master transport is playing) · sync the whole DROID patch to DAW or groovebox · beat-accurate without audio-rate drift.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DROID:
* midiin: channel=1, clock=G9, running=G10

- DAW (MIDI Clock) -> DROID X7 (MIDI IN)
- DROID X7 (G9) c> System Clock (In)
- DROID X7 (G10) g> Transport Gate (In)

patchIdeas.name: SD card edit without removal
patchIdeas.patch: Switch LEFT · SD card appears as USB drive on PC · edit droid.ini in a text editor directly on the card · save · set switch to MIDDLE · patch auto-loads without pressing the master's button · fastest iteration for text-editor patch development.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DROID X7:
* switch: LEFT (USB-stick mode)

- PC (Text Editor) -> DROID X7 (In USB-C) // edit droid.ini
- DROID X7 (Out USB-C) -> DROID (SD Card)
